clinical_coherence.notes: 33F Caucasian with PCOS, anxiety disorder, presenting with psychiatric symptoms progressing to seizures and dyskinesias. Clinical picture is coherent. However, the case shares a very similar template with NMDAR-ENC-M01: same pre-existing anxiety on sertraline, same PMH (PCOS, asthma), same family history pattern, and nearly identical CSF profile (12 WBC, similar protein). The template repetition across moderate cases mirrors the pattern seen in BACT-MEN moderates.

tool_output_consistency.notes: CSF shows mild pleocytosis (12 cells), elevated protein (57.2), borderline glucose (56.8). EEG with extreme delta brush. NMDAR antibodies pending. All outputs concordant and appropriately reflect moderate-difficulty ambiguity.

ground_truth_quality.notes: ICD G04.81 correct. Critical actions and contraindicated actions are clinically appropriate.

difficulty_calibration.notes: Appropriately moderate with pre-existing psychiatric history confounding and mild CSF findings.

demographic_plausibility.notes: 33F Caucasian with PCOS and anxiety is realistic.

educational_value.notes: Substantial overlap with NMDAR-ENC-M01: same pre-existing anxiety template, same mild CSF profile (12 WBC), same clinical progression. Limited incremental educational value.

issues_found: Very similar template to NMDAR-ENC-M01: both have pre-existing anxiety on sertraline, PCOS, identical CSF WBC (12 cells), and same clinical progression pattern
CSF WBC of exactly 12 cells in all three moderate NMDAR cases (M01, M02, M03) is unrealistically consistent
strengths: Sertraline as existing medication is a realistic detail
